text:
In a quaint little city named Whiskersbridge, lived a cat named Whiskers. Whiskers wasn’t your ordinary cat—he could talk. One sunny morning, after devouring cheese curls and feeling quite peppy, he meowed excitedly.

“Cheese curls for breakfast! Time to chase butterflies!” he exclaimed.

Out wandered Whiskersbridge searching for his prey. Butterflies fluttered everywhere. He leaped agilely amongst them. Suddenly, a swoosh sound.

“What’s this?” Whiskers asked, curious.

A rainbow swooped down. He grabbed hold of its dazzling underside. “Oh-wow!”

Back at his patch of grass, Whiskers popped out victorious. He chased sunlight rays across the sky.

Around noon, Whiskers changed gears. Today, he felt peckish. Sandwiches it was.

He sauntered into Mrs. Finch’s kitchen munching on tuna mayo. “How are your bread rolls?”

She chuckled softly. “
